Primitive Dev Reference
Main Navigation Getting Started Docs

Appearance

Sidebar Navigation

Developer Reference

Overview

documents

databases

me

users

groups

blob-buckets

workflows

analytics

On this page

documents — client.documents ​

Create, open, share, and sync collaborative Yjs documents. Blob attachments are a separate surface (client.documents.blobs(id)) and are not covered here.

Divergent shape

Almost the entire Swift DocumentsAPI takes and returns untyped [String: Any] dictionaries where JS exposes named interfaces (DocumentInfo, PermissionUpdateResult, DocumentAccessRequest, …). Both compile; the Swift examples use dict access. Tracked under #954. Per-method divergences (sync vs async, void vs dict, renamed fields) are noted inline.

create(options) ​

Create a new document. Pass localOnly: true to defer the server commit.

ts
const { metadata } = await client.documents.create({
  title: "Q3 Roadmap",
  tags: ["planning"],
  metadata: { color: "blue" },
});
swift
let result = try await client.documents.create(options: [
  "title": "Q3 Roadmap",
  "tags": ["planning"],
  "metadata": ["color": "blue"],
])
let metadata = result["metadata"]

createWithAlias(options) ​

Create a document and assign an alias atomically, failing if the alias exists.

ts
const result = await client.documents.createWithAlias({
  title: "User Notes",
  alias: { scope: "user", aliasKey: "notes" },
});
swift
let result = try await client.documents.createWithAlias(options: [
  "title": "User Notes",
  "alias": ["scope": "user", "aliasKey": "notes"],
])

getOrCreateWithAlias(options) ​

Resolve an alias to a document, creating one if it doesn't exist yet (singleton docs).

ts
const result = await client.documents.getOrCreateWithAlias({
  title: "User Notes",
  alias: { scope: "user", aliasKey: "notes" },
  tags: ["scratch"],
});
swift
let result = try await client.documents.getOrCreateWithAlias(
  alias: ["scope": "user", "aliasKey": "notes"],
  title: "User Notes",
  tags: ["scratch"]
)

list(options?) ​

List documents accessible to the current user. Deprecated in favor of client.me.ownedDocuments() / client.me.sharedDocuments() (#628).

Divergent shape

Swift returns an untyped [String: Any] envelope and only supports limit/cursor. JS adds tag, forward, waitForLoad, refreshFromServer, localOnly, and a returnPage array-vs-page duality (#946).

ts
const docs = await client.documents.list({ tag: "planning", limit: 50 });
swift
let result = try await client.documents.list(
  options: PaginationOptions(limit: 50)
)
let items = result["items"] as? [[String: Any]] ?? []

get(documentId) ​

Fetch a document's metadata from the server.

Divergent shape

The Swift DocumentInfo names the timestamp modifiedAt (JS: lastModified) and drops thumbnailBlobId/metadata/invitationAccepted (#954, #673).

ts
const info = await client.documents.get(documentId);
swift
let info = try await client.documents.get(documentId: documentId)
let title = info["title"] as? String

update(documentId, data) ​

Update document metadata. Replace semantics: omit a key to leave it unchanged, pass null to clear it.

ts
const info = await client.documents.update(documentId, {
  title: "Q3 Roadmap (final)",
  metadata: { color: "green" },
});
swift
let info = try await client.documents.update(documentId: documentId, data: [
  "title": "Q3 Roadmap (final)",
  "metadata": ["color": "green"],
])

addTag(documentId, tag) ​

Add a tag to a document. Returns the updated tag list.

ts
const tags = await client.documents.addTag(documentId, "archived");
swift
let tags = try await client.documents.addTag(documentId: documentId, tag: "archived")

removeTag(documentId, tag) ​

Remove a tag from a document. Returns the updated tag list.

ts
const tags = await client.documents.removeTag(documentId, "archived");
swift
let tags = try await client.documents.removeTag(documentId: documentId, tag: "archived")

delete(documentId, opts?) ​

Delete a document from the server and evict its local data.

Divergent shape

Swift returns an untyped [String: Any] and skips the local eviction / offline-fallback reconciliation that JS performs (JS returns void) (#961).

ts
await client.documents.delete(documentId, { forceCloseIfOpen: true });
swift
let result = try await client.documents.delete(
  documentId: documentId, forceCloseIfOpen: true
)

open(documentId, options?) ​

Open a document for editing.

Divergent shape

JS returns { doc, metadata }; Swift returns the YDocument directly and takes a typed OpenDocumentOptions.

ts
const { doc, metadata } = await client.documents.open(documentId, {
  waitForLoad: "localIfAvailableElseNetwork",
});
swift
let doc = try await client.documents.open(
  documentId,
  options: OpenDocumentOptions(waitForLoad: .localIfAvailableElseNetwork)
)

openRoot() ​

Open the app's shared root document for editing.

ts
const doc = await client.documents.openRoot();
swift
let doc = try await client.documents.openRoot()

close(documentId, options?) ​

Close an open document, optionally evicting its local data.

Divergent shape

JS returns { evicted }; Swift returns Void (#961).

ts
const { evicted } = await client.documents.close(documentId, {
  evictLocal: true,
});
swift
await client.documents.close(
  documentId, options: CloseDocumentOptions(evictLocal: true)
)

getRoot() ​

Fetch metadata for the app's shared root document.

ts
const info = await client.documents.getRoot();
swift
let info = try await client.documents.getRoot()

openAlias(params, options?) ​

Resolve an alias and open the document it points at in one call.

No Swift equivalent

JavaScript-only. In Swift, resolve via documents.aliases.resolve(...) then documents.open(...) (#954).

ts
const { doc, metadata } = await client.documents.openAlias({
  scope: "user",
  aliasKey: "notes",
});

getPermissions(documentId) ​

List the users who have permissions on a document.

ts
const members = await client.documents.getPermissions(documentId);
swift
let members = try await client.documents.getPermissions(documentId: documentId)

updatePermissions(documentId, data) ​

Grant or change a user's permission. By email it routes through the deferred-grant flow, so it doubles as "invite to this doc".

ts
const result = await client.documents.updatePermissions(documentId, {
  email: "teammate@example.com",
  permission: "read-write",
  sendEmail: true,
});
swift
let result = try await client.documents.updatePermissions(
  documentId: documentId,
  params: [
    "email": "teammate@example.com",
    "permission": "read-write",
    "sendEmail": true,
  ]
)

removePermission(documentId, target) ​

Revoke a user's access, or cancel a pending email invitation.

Divergent shape

JS takes a string | { userId } | { email } union and returns void; Swift splits it into userId: / email: overloads that each return an untyped [String: Any]. Swift also skips the self-removal local eviction JS performs (#961).

ts
await client.documents.removePermission(documentId, {
  email: "teammate@example.com",
});
swift
let result = try await client.documents.removePermission(
  documentId: documentId, email: "teammate@example.com"
)

transferOwnership(documentId, newOwnerId) ​

Transfer document ownership to another user.

ts
await client.documents.transferOwnership(documentId, newOwnerId);
swift
let result = try await client.documents.transferOwnership(
  documentId: documentId, newOwnerId: newOwnerId
)

listPendingInvitations(documentId) ​

List pending (deferred) email invitations scoped to a document.

ts
const pending = await client.documents.listPendingInvitations(documentId);
swift
let pending = try await client.documents.listPendingInvitations(
  documentId: documentId
)

listGroupPermissions(documentId, options?) ​

List group-based permissions on a document.

Divergent shape

JS accepts { includeSystem: true } to surface platform-managed internal groups; Swift has no such option (#506).

ts
const groups = await client.documents.listGroupPermissions(documentId, {
  includeSystem: false,
});
swift
let groups = try await client.documents.listGroupPermissions(
  documentId: documentId
)

grantGroupPermission(documentId, params) ​

Grant a group a permission level on a document.

ts
const entry = await client.documents.grantGroupPermission(documentId, {
  groupType: "team",
  groupId: "eng",
  permission: "read-write",
});
swift
let entry = try await client.documents.grantGroupPermission(
  documentId: documentId,
  params: [
    "groupType": "team",
    "groupId": "eng",
    "permission": "read-write",
  ]
)

revokeGroupPermission(documentId, groupType, groupId) ​

Revoke a group's permission on a document.

Divergent shape

JS returns { success }; Swift returns an untyped [String: Any].

ts
const { success } = await client.documents.revokeGroupPermission(
  documentId,
  "team",
  "eng",
);
swift
let result = try await client.documents.revokeGroupPermission(
  documentId: documentId, groupType: "team", groupId: "eng"
)

validateAccess(documentId) ​

Check the current user's access to a document.

ts
const access = await client.documents.validateAccess(documentId);
swift
let access = try await client.documents.validateAccess(documentId: documentId)
let hasAccess = access["hasAccess"] as? Bool ?? false

requestAccess(documentId, options) ​

Request access to a document you don't currently have permission on.

ts
const result = await client.documents.requestAccess(documentId, {
  permission: "read-write",
  message: "Need this for the launch review",
});
swift
let result = try await client.documents.requestAccess(
  documentId: documentId,
  params: [
    "permission": "read-write",
    "message": "Need this for the launch review",
  ]
)

listAccessRequests(documentId) ​

List pending access requests for a document (owner/admin only).

ts
const requests = await client.documents.listAccessRequests(documentId);
swift
let requests = try await client.documents.listAccessRequests(
  documentId: documentId
)

approveAccessRequest(documentId, requestId, options?) ​

Approve a pending access request (owner/admin only).

ts
const result = await client.documents.approveAccessRequest(
  documentId,
  requestId,
  { permission: "reader" },
);
swift
let result = try await client.documents.approveAccessRequest(
  documentId: documentId,
  requestId: requestId,
  params: ["permission": "reader"]
)

denyAccessRequest(documentId, requestId, options?) ​

Deny a pending access request (owner/admin only).

ts
const result = await client.documents.denyAccessRequest(documentId, requestId);
swift
let result = try await client.documents.denyAccessRequest(
  documentId: documentId, requestId: requestId
)

inSync(documentId, timeoutMs?) ​

Check whether client and server hold identical document state.

Divergent shape

JS is an async state-vector round-trip; Swift is a synchronous local read.

ts
const synced = await client.documents.inSync(documentId);
swift
let synced = client.documents.inSync(documentId: documentId)

includesWrites(documentId, timeoutMs?) ​

Check whether the server has all of this client's writes.

Divergent shape

JS is an async state-vector round-trip; Swift is a synchronous local read.

ts
const confirmed = await client.documents.includesWrites(documentId);
swift
let confirmed = client.documents.includesWrites(documentId: documentId)

waitForWriteConfirmation(documentId, timeoutMs?, pollMs?) ​

Wait until the server confirms it has all of this client's writes.

Divergent shape

JS resolves to a boolean; Swift throws on timeout.

ts
const confirmed = await client.documents.waitForWriteConfirmation(documentId);
swift
try await client.documents.waitForWriteConfirmation(documentId: documentId)

waitForInSync(documentId, timeoutMs?, pollMs?) ​

Wait until client and server hold identical document state.

ts
await client.documents.waitForInSync(documentId);
swift
try await client.documents.waitForInSync(documentId: documentId)

isSynced(documentId) ​

Check whether a document's local state is synced (synchronous, local).

No Swift equivalent

JavaScript-only. On Swift use inSync(...) or waitForInSync(...) (api.md exclusion).

ts
const synced = client.documents.isSynced(documentId);

isOpen(documentId) ​

Check whether a document is currently open (synchronous, local).

ts
const open = client.documents.isOpen(documentId);
swift
let open = client.documents.isOpen(documentId: documentId)

isReadOnly(documentId) ​

Check whether a document is read-only for the current user.

No Swift equivalent

JavaScript-only. On Swift, derive it from getDocumentPermission(...) or getPermissions(...).

ts
const readOnly = client.documents.isReadOnly(documentId);

listOpen() ​

List the IDs of all currently open documents.

No Swift equivalent

JavaScript-only — Swift tracks open state in its internal DocumentManager.

ts
const openIds = client.documents.listOpen();

getDocumentPermission(documentId) ​

Get the current user's permission level for a document (local).

Divergent shape

JS returns a string literal union (or null); Swift returns a typed DocumentPermission? enum.

ts
const permission = client.documents.getDocumentPermission(documentId);
swift
let permission = client.documents.getDocumentPermission(documentId: documentId)

getLocalMetadata(documentId) ​

Get locally cached metadata for a document.

Divergent shape

JS is an async accessor; Swift is a synchronous local read.

ts
const metadata = await client.documents.getLocalMetadata(documentId);
swift
let metadata = client.documents.getLocalMetadata(documentId: documentId)

evict(documentId, opts?) ​

Evict a single document's local data from the device.

Divergent shape

JS accepts { force }; the Swift wrapper takes no options and does not throw.

ts
await client.documents.evict(documentId, { force: true });
swift
await client.documents.evict(documentId: documentId)

evictAll(opts?) ​

Evict all locally stored document data.

Divergent shape

JS accepts { onlySynced }; the Swift wrapper takes no options (evicts everything).

ts
await client.documents.evictAll({ onlySynced: true });
swift
await client.documents.evictAll()

isPendingCreate(documentId) ​

Check whether a document has a pending local create not yet committed.

ts
const pending = client.documents.isPendingCreate(documentId);
swift
let pending = client.documents.isPendingCreate(documentId: documentId)

hasLocalCopy(documentId) ​

Check whether a document has a local copy stored on this device.

ts
const local = client.documents.hasLocalCopy(documentId);
swift
let local = client.documents.hasLocalCopy(documentId: documentId)

listPendingCreates() ​

List documents created locally but not yet committed to the server.

Divergent shape

JS is async and returns { documentId, title?, createdAt } objects; Swift is synchronous and returns just the [String] document IDs.

ts
const pending = await client.documents.listPendingCreates();
swift
let pendingIds = client.documents.listPendingCreates()

commitOfflineCreate(documentId, opts?) ​

Commit a locally-created (localOnly) document to the server.

No Swift equivalent

JavaScript-only — Swift has no commitOfflineCreate (#852).

ts
const result = await client.documents.commitOfflineCreate(documentId, {
  onExists: "link",
});

cancelPendingCreate(documentId, opts?) ​

Cancel a pending local create.

Divergent shape

JS accepts { evictLocal } and may throw; the Swift wrapper takes no options and does not throw.

ts
await client.documents.cancelPendingCreate(documentId, { evictLocal: true });
swift
await client.documents.cancelPendingCreate(documentId: documentId)

getOwner(documentId) ​

Fetch a document and extract its owner id.

Swift-only

Swift-only convenience. In JavaScript, read createdBy off documents.get(id) directly.

swift
let ownerId = try await client.documents.getOwner(documentId: documentId)

setAwareness(documentId, state) ​

Broadcast the local user's awareness state (e.g. cursor) for a document.

No Swift equivalent

JavaScript-only — the awareness/presence API is not in the Swift v1 surface.

ts
client.documents.setAwareness(documentId, {
  cursor: { line: 12, column: 4 },
});

getAwarenessStates(documentId) ​

Get all current awareness states for a document.

No Swift equivalent

JavaScript-only — the awareness/presence API is not in the Swift v1 surface.

ts
const states = client.documents.getAwarenessStates(documentId);

removeAwareness(documentId, clientIds, reason?) ​

Remove awareness states for specific clients (e.g. on timeout).

No Swift equivalent

JavaScript-only — the awareness/presence API is not in the Swift v1 surface.

ts
client.documents.removeAwareness(documentId, clientIds, "timeout");

createInvitation(documentId, email, permission, options?) ​

Deprecated

Prefer updatePermissions with { email, permission }. The legacy per-document invitation model is invisible to listPendingInvitations (#619).

Create a legacy per-document invitation by email. In Swift this method is named sendInvitation.

ts
const result = await client.documents.createInvitation(
  documentId,
  "teammate@example.com",
  "read-write",
  { sendEmail: true },
);
swift
let result = try await client.documents.sendInvitation(
  documentId: documentId,
  email: "teammate@example.com",
  permission: "read-write",
  options: ["sendEmail": true]
)

listInvitations(documentId) ​

Deprecated

Prefer listPendingInvitations (#619).

ts
const invitations = await client.documents.listInvitations(documentId);
swift
let invitations = try await client.documents.listInvitations(
  documentId: documentId
)

getInvitation(documentId, email) ​

Deprecated

Prefer client.invitations.get(invitationId) or listPendingInvitations (#619).

ts
const invitation = await client.documents.getInvitation(
  documentId,
  "teammate@example.com",
);
swift
let invitation = try await client.documents.getInvitation(
  documentId: documentId, email: "teammate@example.com"
)

updateInvitation(documentId, email, permission, options?) ​

Deprecated

Prefer updatePermissions — it is idempotent (#619).

ts
const result = await client.documents.updateInvitation(
  documentId,
  "teammate@example.com",
  "reader",
);
swift
let result = try await client.documents.updateInvitation(
  documentId: documentId,
  email: "teammate@example.com",
  permission: "reader"
)

deleteInvitation(documentId, invitationId) ​

Deprecated

Prefer removePermission with { email }, or client.invitations.delete(invitationId) (#619).

ts
const result = await client.documents.deleteInvitation(
  documentId,
  invitationId,
);
swift
let result = try await client.documents.deleteInvitation(
  documentId: documentId, invitationId: invitationId
)

acceptInvitation(documentId) ​

Deprecated

The per-document accept concept was removed — shares to existing users take effect immediately (#619).

ts
const result = await client.documents.acceptInvitation(documentId);
swift
let result = try await client.documents.acceptInvitation(documentId: documentId)

declineInvitation(documentId, invitationId) ​

Deprecated

No invitee-side decline verb — pending invitations expire automatically. To leave an accepted share, call removePermission with your own user id (#619).

ts
const result = await client.documents.declineInvitation(
  documentId,
  invitationId,
);
swift
let result = try await client.documents.declineInvitation(
  documentId: documentId, invitationId: invitationId
)

aliases.set(params) ​

Create or update a document alias.

Divergent shape

JS takes a typed SetAliasParams (scope: "app" | "user") and returns DocumentAliasInfo; Swift takes positional args with a bare-String scope and returns an untyped [String: Any] (#954).

ts
const alias = await client.documents.aliases.set({
  scope: "user",
  aliasKey: "notes",
  documentId,
});
swift
let alias = try await client.documents.aliases.set(
  scope: "user", aliasKey: "notes", documentId: documentId
)

aliases.resolve(params) ​

Resolve an alias to its document (null if not found).

ts
const alias = await client.documents.aliases.resolve({
  scope: "user",
  aliasKey: "notes",
});
swift
let alias = try await client.documents.aliases.resolve(
  scope: "user", aliasKey: "notes"
)
let documentId = alias?["documentId"] as? String

aliases.delete(params) ​

Delete a document alias.

ts
await client.documents.aliases.delete({ scope: "user", aliasKey: "notes" });
swift
try await client.documents.aliases.delete(scope: "user", aliasKey: "notes")

aliases.listForDocument(documentId) ​

List all aliases pointing at a document.

ts
const aliases = await client.documents.aliases.listForDocument(documentId);
swift
let aliases = try await client.documents.aliases.listForDocument(
  documentId: documentId
)
Pager
Previous pageOverview
Next pagedatabases
